macro_segments.title: The Status of Unspecified Funds and Animals with Blemishes in Nazir Sacrifices

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Sugya: Pile of Unspecified Funds for Nazir Offerings
Debate: Does the Halacha of Unspecified Funds Apply Only to Money or to Other Objects
Animals with Blemishes as Candidates for the Unspecified Funds Category

macro_segments.title: Rava's Challenge: The Braita on Chatas Specificity and Personal Obligation

micro_segments.title: Rava's Opening Question: Korban Must Be for One's Own Sin
Braita: Cannot Use Father's Chatas Even if Money and Within Same Category
Reading the Braita: Chatas Requires Specificity to Person and Sin
Cannot Reassign Even Own Money for Chatas from One Sin to Another
Tosafot: Money Loses Sanctity More Readily Through Me'ila Be'Shogeg
The Braita's Relevance: Contrasting Flexibility of Money vs. Specified Animals

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Resolution: All Non-Sacrificial Objects Equal to Money

micro_segments.title: Gemara Questions Whether Blemished Animals Belong to Money Category
Answer: All Non-Sacrificial Objects Fall into the Money Category

macro_segments.title: Woman Nazir's Annulment When Husband Appears During Sacrifice

micro_segments.title: Mishna: After First Blood is Sprinkled Husband Cannot Annul
Rabbi Akiva: Cannot Annul Even After Slaughter Due to Hefsed Kodashim
Tanna Kamma vs. Rabbi Akiva: At What Stage Can Husband No Longer Annul

macro_segments.title: Annulment of Woman Nazir After Becoming Tamei and During Final Shaving

micro_segments.title: If Woman Becomes Tamei: Full Month Remains, Husband Can Still Annul Anytime
Rabbi Meir: Can Annul Even During Final Shaving to Prevent Disfigurement

macro_segments.title: Why Chatas Specifically Matters: Shelo Lishma and Loss of Sanctity

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Zeira's Challenge: Olah and Shelamim Can Still Be Brought Shelo Lishma
Shelo Lishma: Wrong Purpose Does Not Invalidate Non-Chatas Sacrifices
Gemara Clarifies: Concern for Hefsed Kodashim Applies Only to Chatas

macro_segments.title: Annulment of Woman Nazir After Becoming Tamei and During Final Shaving (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma's Objection: Wig Alternative and Rabbi Meir's Response on Hair Smell
